challenges.title: Symbol ID: Position
challenges.question: Identify the geometric characteristic symbol shown.
challenges.explanation: The crosshair inside a circle is the Position symbol. It locates a feature of size relative to datums.
challenges.asset_svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 88 80" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" font-family="'Share Tech Mono', ui-monospace, monospace"><circle cx="44" cy="40" r="11" fill="none" stroke="#00f0ff" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="28" y1="40" x2="60" y2="40" stroke="#00f0ff" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="44" y1="24" x2="44" y2="56" stroke="#00f0ff" stroke-width="2"/></svg>

challenges.title: Symbol ID: Flatness
challenges.question: Identify the geometric characteristic symbol shown.
challenges.explanation: The parallelogram is Flatness, a form control. Form controls take no datum reference.
challenges.asset_svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 88 80" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" font-family="'Share Tech Mono', ui-monospace, monospace"><polygon points="32,47.2 40.4,32.8 56,32.8 47.6,47.2" fill="none" stroke="#00f0ff" stroke-width="2"/></svg>

challenges.title: Symbol ID: Perpendicularity
challenges.question: Identify the geometric characteristic symbol shown.
challenges.explanation: The inverted-T symbol is Perpendicularity, an orientation control holding a feature 90 degrees to a datum.
challenges.asset_svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 88 80" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" font-family="'Share Tech Mono', ui-monospace, monospace"><line x1="41.6" y1="28" x2="41.6" y2="52" stroke="#00f0ff" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="32" y1="52" x2="56" y2="52" stroke="#00f0ff" stroke-width="2"/></svg>

challenges.title: Feature Control Frame Anatomy
challenges.question: In a feature control frame, what does the first (leftmost) compartment always contain?
challenges.explanation: A frame reads left to right: characteristic symbol, then tolerance, then datum references.

challenges.title: Basic Dimensions
challenges.question: A dimension enclosed in a rectangular box (a basic dimension) represents what?
choices: A theoretically exact value with no tolerance of its own
A reference dimension that may be ignored
A toleranced dimension of plus or minus 0.001
challenges.answer: A theoretically exact value with no tolerance of its own
challenges.explanation: A basic dimension is exact. The permitted variation comes from the feature control frame, not the dimension.

challenges.title: Plus/Minus Tolerance: Max Size
challenges.question: A dimension is 1.000 +/- 0.005 in. What is the maximum acceptable size?
challenges.explanation: Maximum is the nominal plus the upper tolerance: 1.000 + 0.005 = 1.005.

challenges.title: Tolerance Zone Shape
challenges.question: The feature control frame shown is applied to a hole. What is the shape and size of its tolerance zone?
challenges.explanation: The diameter symbol ahead of the tolerance makes the zone cylindrical, 0.010 across.

challenges.title: Datum Precedence
challenges.question: In the feature control frame shown, which datum is the primary datum?
challenges.explanation: Datums are listed in order of precedence. The first datum compartment, A, is primary and is contacted first.

challenges.title: What Perpendicularity Controls
challenges.question: What does the feature control frame shown control?
choices: Orientation of the feature at 90 degrees to datum A within a 0.005 zone
Location of the feature from datum A
challenges.answer: Orientation of the feature at 90 degrees to datum A within a 0.005 zone
challenges.explanation: Perpendicularity is an orientation control. It does not locate or size the feature.

challenges.title: Diametral Clearance
challenges.question: A hole is dia 0.510 and the shaft assembled into it is dia 0.500. What is the diametral clearance?
challenges.explanation: Clearance is hole minus shaft: 0.510 - 0.500 = 0.010.

challenges.title: MMC of a Hole
challenges.question: A hole is dimensioned dia 0.500 to 0.510. What is its maximum material condition (MMC)?
challenges.explanation: A hole has the most material when it is smallest, so MMC is the 0.500 limit.

challenges.title: Identify the Fit
challenges.question: A hole is dia 0.500 to 0.504 and a shaft is dia 0.495 to 0.498. What type of fit is this?
challenges.explanation: The hole is always larger than the shaft (min clearance 0.002, max 0.009), so it is always a clearance fit.

challenges.title: Bonus Tolerance
challenges.question: A hole dia 0.500 to 0.520 has the position control shown, applied at MMC. The hole is produced at dia 0.515. What is the total position tolerance available (zone diameter)?
challenges.explanation: Bonus = produced size - MMC = 0.515 - 0.500 = 0.015. Total = stated 0.010 + bonus 0.015 = 0.025.

challenges.title: Runout Type
challenges.question: Which control inspects the entire cylindrical surface as the part rotates, not just individual circular cross sections?
challenges.explanation: Circular runout checks one cross section at a time; total runout controls the whole surface at once.

challenges.title: Degrees of Freedom
challenges.question: A primary planar datum feature, when contacted, removes how many degrees of freedom?
challenges.explanation: A plane stops one translation (normal to it) and two rotations, removing three of the six degrees of freedom.

challenges.title: Profile Disposition
challenges.question: A profile of a surface tolerance of 0.020 is shown equally disposed about the true profile. How far may the surface deviate to either side of the true profile?
challenges.explanation: Equally disposed splits the 0.020 zone in half, so 0.010 to each side.

challenges.title: Why Use MMC
challenges.question: Why does adding the MMC modifier to a position tolerance benefit production?
choices: It allows bonus tolerance as the feature departs from MMC
It tightens the tolerance as the hole grows
It changes the zone from round to square
challenges.answer: It allows bonus tolerance as the feature departs from MMC
challenges.explanation: As the hole grows away from MMC, extra position tolerance becomes available, easing manufacture without hurting assembly.

challenges.title: Virtual Condition: Pin
challenges.question: A pin (external feature) has an MMC of dia 0.500 and a perpendicularity tolerance of 0.005 applied at MMC. What is its virtual condition?
challenges.explanation: For an external feature, virtual condition = MMC + geometric tolerance = 0.500 + 0.005 = 0.505.
challenges.asset_svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 195 74" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" font-family="'Share Tech Mono', ui-monospace, monospace"><rect x="16" y="16" width="163" height="42" fill="none" stroke="#e8ffe8" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="32.6" y1="25.0" x2="32.6" y2="49.0" stroke="#00f0ff" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="23.0" y1="49.0" x2="47.0" y2="49.0" stroke="#00f0ff" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="54" y1="16" x2="54" y2="58" stroke="#e8ffe8" stroke-width="2"/><text x="68" y="37.0" fill="#e8ffe8" font-family="'Share Tech Mono', ui-monospace, monospace" font-size="16" text-anchor="start" dominant-baseline="central">0.005</text><circle cx="125" cy="37.0" r="9" fill="none" stroke="#c8ff00" stroke-width="2"/><text x="125" y="37.0" fill="#c8ff00" font-family="'Share Tech Mono', ui-monospace, monospace" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">M</text><line x1="149" y1="16" x2="149" y2="58" stroke="#e8ffe8" stroke-width="2"/><text x="164" y="37.0" fill="#00ff41" font-family="'Share Tech Mono', ui-monospace, monospace" font-size="16" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">A</text></svg>

challenges.title: Virtual Condition: Hole
challenges.question: A hole (internal feature) has an MMC of dia 0.510 and a position tolerance of 0.010 applied at MMC. What is its virtual condition?
challenges.explanation: For an internal feature, virtual condition = MMC - geometric tolerance = 0.510 - 0.010 = 0.500.

challenges.title: Worst-Case Assembly
challenges.question: A hole has a virtual condition of dia 0.500 and the pin going into it has a virtual condition of dia 0.500. What does this guarantee?
choices: The parts will always just assemble at worst case
The parts will always have 0.010 clearance
challenges.answer: The parts will always just assemble at worst case
challenges.explanation: Matching virtual conditions define the worst-case mating boundary. Equal VCs mean the pin and hole always just fit.
